name: Djerba Seawater Desalination Plant
summary: A major seawater desalination facility serving Djerba and reducing pressure on local groundwater and transferred water resources.
supplyText: The plant adds a large non-conventional water source for Djerba, where local groundwater resources are stressed and tourism demand increases seasonal pressure.
energyText: The plant should be treated as part of the water-energy nexus because seawater desalination increases electricity demand. Energy source and specific energy consumption should be verified before publication.
institutionalText: This entry should be used to show how desalination shifts water security from groundwater availability toward infrastructure finance, electricity supply, and network management.

name: Zarat / Gabès Desalination Plant
beneficiaryMethod: programme-level figures only; plant-level beneficiaries not yet verified
summary: A strategic southern desalination project whose expansion is linked to water security improvements in Gabès, Médenine, and Tataouine.
supplyText: The expansion is intended to increase desalinated water production for southern Tunisia and improve drinking water reliability across several governorates.
energyText: The plant should be framed as a water security intervention with energy-system consequences. Brine and electricity implications should remain visibly marked as estimates until source-specific figures are verified.
institutionalText: This project is useful for explaining the institutional geography of desalination: coastal production, inland distribution, external finance, and SONEDE network performance.
